title: sterculia caribaea
intro: Sterculia caribaea, also known as the West Indian sterculia, is a tree species native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the family Malvaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Sterculia caribaea are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and roots of the plant are typically used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
